revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Atagozuka Kofun]][[ja:愛宕塚古墳 (八尾市)]][[zh:愛宕塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Atagozuka Kohun, Yao01.jpg|location={{ill|Yao|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.636183333333|N|135.646325|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}}}

{{short description|Tomb in Osaka, Japan|qq=}}
{{more sources|date=May 2017|qq=}}
{{Coord|34|38|10.43|N|135|38|47.42|E|display=title|qq=}}
{{Nihongo|'''Atagozuka Kofun'''|愛宕塚古墳|"Atagozuka ancient tomb"|qq=}} is a mounded tomb located in the Kōdachi area of {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|qid=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka|1=Yao, Osaka}}, in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref name="Sawa">{{cite journal|last1=Sawa|first1=Isao|title=Osaka University of Economics and Law  West of Atagozuka-Kofun (Cave)|journal=洞窟環境NET学会紀要|issue=6|url=http://www.cave-ens.com/pdf/CENS6_014.pdf|accessdate=11 December 2016|qq=}}</ref> It is the largest scallop-shaped Kofun, or ''[[kofun]]''. It is a round Kofun 22.5 meters in diameter and 9 meters tall. It was constructed in the late sixth century, during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}.
==Later use ==
Atagozuka-kofun existed as a grave in the late 6th century and hosted additional burials. After that it also existed as a sacred place for some 200 years. The tomb was reused after the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} as shown by the excavation of two pieces of [[:en:sueki|sueki]] (unglazed ware) of the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} and also of black pottery, unglazed pottery, {{ill|Hagi ware|en|Hagi ware|qid=Q1568647|lt=Hagi|1=Hagi ware}} plates and [[hagama]] (kama with wing) of the middleand later Heian and {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}} periods. An excavated five-ring {{ill|Pagoda|en|Pagoda|qid=Q199451|lt=Pagoda|1=Pagoda}} and human bones of the [[Muromachi]] period show that this ancient tomb was used for additional burials in the medieval period.
==References==
